I-94 construction, upgrading in Minneapolis and Brooklyn Center, Hennepin County : environmental impact statement.

The proposal involves the construction and upgrading of a segment of Interstate 94 (I-94) in Minneapolis and Brooklyn Center, Minnesota. The project aims to improve transportation capacity and safety by constructing a new eight-lane freeway and upgrading existing sections. This development is intended to enhance connectivity in the Minneapolis-St. Paul metropolitan area, facilitating better interstate and local travel. However, it will impact parklands, displace residents and businesses, and increase noise levels. The project includes measures to mitigate these effects, such as noise abatement and replacement of parklands.
Minneapolis and Brooklyn Center, Hennepin County, Minnesota
Metropolitan Council of the Twin Cities Area

The I-94 construction and upgrading project in Minneapolis and Brooklyn Center was proposed to address growing transportation needs in the Minneapolis-St. Paul metropolitan area. While the project aimed to improve safety and capacity, it faced public pushback due to concerns over environmental impacts, including the displacement of residents and businesses, as well as the loss of parklands. Ultimately, the project proceeded with plans for mitigation measures to address these concerns.

"The City of Brooklyn Center has invested both land and planning in developing the industrial park at I-94 and Shingle Creek Parkway. This industrial park location is consistent with metropolitan area planning." — City of Brooklyn Center

"The proposed action will not generate any additional demand for social services. The corridor has been selected to enhance neighborhood cohesion and to separate conflicting land uses." — Section I, Part I – Need for the Proposed Action
